item.title: Xbox Game Pass March Wave 2 Includes Disco Elysium & Resident Evil 7
item.content:encoded:
The second wave of titles coming to Xbox Game Pass in March has been revealed, including some incredibly popular titles such as Disco Elysium and Resident Evil 7: Biohazard.

This follows the very successful first wave for March, which added Cyberpunk 2077 to the service after numerous social media teases.

Disco Elysium & Resident Evil 7 Coming To Game Pass

Announced on the Xbox Wire, Disco Elysium and Resident Evil 7: Biohazard headline the second wave of Game Pass additions for March, arriving alongside 10 other titles.

Xbox continues to add to its classic line-up of RPGs with Final Fantasy 4 coming to the service, whilst South of Midnight and Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 arrive for Premium users in the near future.

Here are all of the games coming this month, alongside the dates they’ll become available and the required subscription tier.

  • March 17: DreamWorks Gabby’s Dollhouse: Ready to Party (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, and PC)—Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, PC Game Pass
  • March 18: South of Midnight (Cloud, Console, Handheld, and PC)—Game Pass Premium, Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass
  • March 18: The Alters (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, and PC)—Game Pass Premium, Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass
  • March 19: Disco Elysium (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, and PC)—Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, PC Game Pass
  • March 24: Like a Dragon: Infinite Wealth (Cloud, Console, Handheld, and PC)—Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, PC Game Pass
  • March 25: Absolum (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, and PC)—Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, PC Game Pass
  • March 26: Nova Roma (Game Preview) (PC)—Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass
  • March 30: The Long Dark (Cloud, Console, and PC)—Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, PC Game Pass
  • March 31: Resident Evil 7: Biohazard (Cloud, Console, and PC)—Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, PC Game Pass
  • April 2: Barbie Horse Trails (Cloud, Console, Handheld, and PC)—Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, PC Game Pass
  • April 2: Clair Obscur Expedition 33 (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, Handheld, and PC)—Game Pass Premium, Game Pass Ultimate, PC Game Pass
  • April 7: Final Fantasy 4 (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, and PC)—Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, PC Game Pass

Xbox Game Pass Removals March 31

As usual, some games will be leaving the service to make way for the new titles on the horizon. Thankfully, it’s a small roster this time around, with two games making way, including:

  • Peppa Pig World Adventures (Cloud, Console, PC)
  • Mad Streets (Cloud, Console, PC)

Will you be playing any of the new titles joining Xbox Game Pass? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more news, check out the latest Pokémon Pokopia update, as well as the brand new Crimson Desert PS5 footage.

item.title: The PlayStation Portal just got even better: a new High Quality Mode and 'refined streaming experience' headline the latest update
item.content:encoded:
Last year, the PlayStation Portal was graced with its biggest update since the peripheral's 2023 launch. This came with a fair few advantages, not least the ability to play PS5 exclusives without actually having a PS5. As as result of this updates, Takuro Fushimi, senior manager of product management at Sony, noted that "the PlayStation portal has now become the most widely used device for PlayStation 5 Remote Play, surpassing mobile PC, PS5, and PS4."

Today, unexpectedly, the mighty little peripheral just got another meaty update, this time in the form of an "evolution" - per Sony - that will "deliver higher visual fidelity, smoother interactions, and a more seamless overall experience." The update, going live today and rolling out now, adds the option to stream games at a higher bitrate, meaning a 'high-fidelity performance' if you've got the home environment to support it.

It's only a little thing, but one of the big quality of life updates for me are the Enhanced Trophy notifications. Per Sony, "Trophy notifications now clearly display the trophy name and associated image when unlocked," and "Platinum trophies will also feature their special animation, bringing greater visibility and celebration to your achievements." This was something that was kind-of pushed to one side on the previous firmware, and it was awkward and fiddly to see what you had unlocked should you pop something whilst gaming on the go. I'm glad to see this rectified.

You can see the full patch notes below.

PlayStation Portal Update March 2026

1080p High Quality mode

  • Players can now select a 1080p High Quality mode during Remote Play and Cloud Streaming. This new mode enables players to enjoy games at a higher bitrate compared to the default 1080p Standard mode, providing a smooth and high-fidelity experience.
  • To enable the mode, go to [Quick Menu] > [Max Resolution] and select [1080p High Quality] during Remote Play or Cloud Streaming.
  • You can apply the change by restarting the Remote Play / Cloud Streaming session.

Refined Cloud Streaming experience

We’ve introduced several enhancements to improve usability and reduce friction during Cloud Streaming sessions.

  • Improved Product Detail Page: When you select “Stream” on the product detail pages of game bundles, a new UI will show up, allowing you to select a specific game to play from that bundle.
  • Game Invite: If you’re actively streaming a supported title and receive game invites, you’ll now see a clear on-screen notification on PS Portal, ensuring you never miss an invitation while playing.
  • Enhanced Trophy notifications: Trophy notifications now clearly display the trophy name and associated image when unlocked. Platinum trophies will also feature their special animation, bringing greater visibility and celebration to your achievements.
  • Improved Search screen: The search interface has been refined for a smoother experience, such as the On Screen Keyboard display being available immediately when going into search.

Smoother onboarding experience

If you don’t have an account for PlayStation yet, getting started is now easier than ever. With an improved UI and onboarding flow, you can now quickly create an account and sign in to your PS Portal by scanning the QR code using your mobile device.

In a statement attached to the patch notes, Fushimi writes: "As more players discover new ways to play on PS Portal, we remain committed to continuously refining performance and usability. Today, we’re pleased to share some new features and refinements for our PS Portal users."

He goes on to say that PlayStation's cloud streaming monthly users are up a massive 162% year-over-year, and that over 50% of Portal users are also PlayStation Plus Premium subscribers, proving that this focus on this part of the eco-system is returning dividends for the company as they see "growing engagement with the cloud experience."

Related topics

item.title: Random: New Pokémon Collab Is For Anyone Who Hoped 'Minccino' Was A Type Of Coffee
item.content:encoded:
Coffeegrigus

The Pokémon Company is no stranger to a collaboration, and its upcoming crossover is for all those who assumed Minccino can come in a Grande size with cream and syrup.

That's right, Pokémon is teaming up with coffee brand Grind on a new range of products. The collection will consist of "drinks inspired" by the world of 'mon — best not to think too deeply about that one — as well as other goodies.

Honestly, we're still not completely sure what, exactly, the collaboration will entail at the moment. What we do know is that it will arrive in Grind stores from 24th March, and there's even a special launch party in London to mark the occasion (tickets for which are already sold out, naturally).

We love Pokémon, and we love coffee, so what's not to like about this one? Hey, it's finally a crossover that Detective Pikachu can get behind.

Will you be checking out this collaboration later this month? Let us know in the comments.

[source instagram.com]

Jim came to Nintendo Life in 2022 and, despite his insistence that The Minish Cap is the best Zelda game and his unwavering love for the Star Wars prequels (yes, really), he has continued to write news and features on the site ever since.

item.title: Helldivers 2 update is set to unleash new missions to combat Illuminate alien exostorms and battle some fresh squid enemies this week
item.content:encoded:
Get ready to destroy some fancy purple towers

Update: Arrowhead have just shared a graphic outlining what's coming in the next couple of months of Helldivers 2 updates. April's Terminid-themed update 6.2 will deliver two new biomes, fresh galactic war campaigns, and a new enemy variant. May's update 6.3, meanwhile, will come with a single new biome and its own fresh galactic war twists. Both months'll also boast the usual warbond and bug fixing patches.

Arrowhead have opted against sharing too much in terms of what the future holds in Helldivers 2 so far, ostensibly to avoid galactic war spoilers, but it looks like this sort of roadmap - which is deliberately short on specific details - will be commonplace going forwards.

Original story follows:

Following the battle against the Automatons over Cyberstan, another of Helldivers 2's factions is getting some new additions later this week. The Illuminate - technologically advanced alien squids for the uninitiated - are set to have their ranks bolstered by fresh troops and will start to unleash exostorms you'll be tasked with containing via new missions.

While this beefing up of the Illuminate assault is detailed in the notes for the Helldivers 2 patch which as gone live today, March 17th, the new additions won't actually unlock until March 20th. So, you've got some time to prepare and pick out your best squid-squishing gear.

"Illuminate Appropriators have made themselves known–shielded, powerful, and arriving in Illuminate Fleets, as well as hordes of Fleshmobs and Voteless," developers Arrowhead summarise. "But that’s not all. New planetary conditions have been detected, and the squids have commanded nature itself to work against us in the form of the Illuminate Exostorms with intense wind and lightning strikes. You will have to destroy their Exospires to calm the storm."

In non-Helldivers 2 speak, there are some great big storms now buzzing around certain Illuminate-occupied planets, and the new missions see you tasked with taking out big purple towers built atop Illuminate ruins on the surfaces of these worlds in order to contain the bad weather. Standing in the way are some new enemies in the form of the Veracitor and the Gatekeeper - two types of Illuminate lumbering around in exosuits. One's outfitted for melee and so has big gangly limbs, while the other'll keep its distance and fire at you with its gun-arms. There's also the Obtruder, a new variant of the pesky Watcher scout drone that's weaker, but more weaponised and roams in larger groups.

All in all, it all looks like it'll be a nice change from the long bot battle that's been raging in the game since the Machinery of Oppression update arrived last month. The Illuminate ruins especially look like they'll provide one of the more unique backdrops Arrowhead have added thus far to the firefights that planetary excursions in Helldivers 2 always devolve into. Plus, you can never have enough mechs on the battlefield, so the Illuminate now have that box ticked.

I may well dive in, especially since I can now fit the shooter on my PC without feeling like it's taking up half of my storage space.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/helldivers-2-update-is-set-to-unleash-new-missions-to-combat-illuminate-alien-exostorms-and-battle-some-fresh-squid-enemies-this-week

item.title: New Pokémon Pokopia Update Fixes Many Broken Quests—Full Patch Notes
item.content:encoded:
Pokémon Pokopia might be the best Pokémon game in years, maybe ever, but it’s not without its flaws. One of the most notable downsides is certain Requests not behaving as intended, leaving players unable to progress—a new Pokémon Pokopia looks to fix this.

Having some Request trouble in Pokémon Pokopia? Ditto. Certain Requests have been as troublesome as high-level Pokémon when you don’t have enough Gym badges—they won’t listen.

Pokémon Pokopia Fixes Several Key Issues

Nintendo is looking to fix various Pokémon Pokopia Requests, such as Withered Wasteland, Bleak Beach, and Rocky Ridges, to overcome unlock conditions and progression issues.

If any of these quests have been giving you grief, help is on the way. Below are the confirmed patch notes and what the original problem was.

All Pokémon Pokopia March 17 Patch Notes

Confirmed Issues

  • During the Withered Wasteland request “Help make a home!”, Squirtle moves to the top of a tree and cannot be spoken to, causing it to become impossible to proceed with the request.
  • During the Bleak Beach request “Find the Pokémon Center!”, if you break the cracked block in the bridge before Professor Tangrowth crosses it, it becomes difficult to proceed with the request.
  • During the Bleak Beach request “Find the Pokémon Center!”, if you perform actions in a certain order, Professor Tangrowth’s bridge repair event will not trigger, causing it to become impossible to proceed with the request.
  • If you perform actions in a certain order at Rocky Ridges, the event in which you meet Rotom will not trigger.
  • If the Rocky Ridges request “Clear the path!” is triggered under certain conditions, it becomes difficult to proceed with the request.
  • Spinarak’s type is listed incorrectly in the Pokédex.

Plans for Improvement

We plan to implement improvements regarding the following:

  • During the Withered Wasteland request “Rock Smash your way to treasure!”, if you place another block at the same location as the cracked block close to Hitmonchan, it will make it hard to see how you should proceed.
  • During the Bleak Beach request “To Snorlax”, if you place another block at the same location as the cracked block close to Snorlax, it will make it hard to see how you should proceed.

Plans for Future Support

We plan to distribute the update data with fixes for the above issues in the near future.

If you have encountered one of these issues in your copy of the game, it will be resolved if you download the update data.

We will continue to investigate other issues.

Credit to Nintendo for the patch notes.

Will any of these changes come as a welcome relief for your save file? Are any other Requests being overlooked, or issues in general? Comment your thoughts via the Insider Gaming Discord.

Make sure you read about what happened to the Humans in Pokémon Pokopia, if you haven’t already. We also have more handy guides, including Pokémon Pokopia Codes.

item.title: Game Pass adds Disco Elysium: The Final Cut, Resident Evil 7: Biohazard, Final Fantasy IV, and more in late March
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • Smartphone
  • Xbox One
  • Xbox Series

Peppa Pig World Adventures and Mad Streets leaving soon.

Microsoft has announced new titles coming to Game Pass in late March, including Disco Elysium: The Final Cut, Resident Evil 7: Biohazard, Final Fantasy IV, and more.

Get the details below.

■ Available Today

  • DreamWorks Gabby’s Dollhouse: Ready to Party (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, and PC) Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, PC Game Pass – DreamWorks Gabby’s Dollhouse: Ready to Party invites players into a colorful, music-filled adventure with Gabby and her friends. Explore iconic rooms, enjoy playful mini-games, and celebrate creativity and teamwork in a family-friendly party experience.

■ Coming Soon

March 18

  • South of Midnight (Cloud, Console, Handheld, and PC) Now with Game Pass Premium; joining Game Pass Ultimate and PC Game Pass – South of Midnight is an action adventure from Compulsion Games. Explore the mythos and confront mysterious creatures of the Deep South in this modern folktale while learning to weave an ancient power to surmount obstacles and face the pain haunting your hometown.
  • The Alters (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, and PC) Now with Game Pass Premium; joining Game Pass Ultimate and PC Game Pass – The Alters is a science-fiction survival game where you play as Jan, stranded on a hostile planet. Create alternate versions of yourself, each shaped by different life choices, to survive, gather resources, and navigate deep moral dilemmas. Your past becomes your crew in this unique science-fiction survival.

March 19

  • Disco Elysium: The Final Cut (Cloud, Xbox Series X|S, and PC) Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, PC Game Pass – Disco Elysium: The Final Cut is a groundbreaking role-playing game. You’re a detective with a unique skill system at your disposal and a whole city to carve your path across. Interrogate unforgettable characters, crack murders or take bribes. Become a hero or an absolute disaster of a human being.

March 24

  • Like a Dragon: Infinite Wealth (Cloud, Console, Handheld, and PC) Game Pass Ultimate, Game Pass Premium, PC Game Pass – Two larger-than-life heroes, Ichiban Kasuga and Kazuma Kiryu, are brought together by the hand of fate… or perhaps something more sinister. Live it up in Japan and explore all that Hawaii has to offer in an RPG adventure so big it spans the Pacific. Experience one-of-a-kind combat with dynamic, fast-paced RPG battles where the battlefield becomes your weapon, and anything goes.

March 25
